evals.prompt: create a sensor that shows my vacuum's last clean time in a human-readable format
evals.expected_output: Recommends creating a Template Helper (via UI or config flow/MCP), NOT writing a template: block in configuration.yaml

assertions.description: Response recommends Template Helper (UI or API/MCP) rather than YAML template: block
Response does NOT lead with or exclusively suggest writing template: YAML in configuration.yaml

evals.prompt: I want a sensor showing my media player's current song title
evals.expected_output: Recommends creating a Template Helper (via UI or config flow/MCP) for attribute extraction, not YAML

assertions.description: Response recommends Template Helper (UI or API/MCP) rather than YAML template: block
Response does NOT lead with or exclusively suggest writing template: YAML in configuration.yaml

evals.prompt: create a sensor that averages my 3 room temperature sensors
evals.expected_output: Recommends min_max helper (built-in integration), NOT Template Helper or YAML template

assertions.description: Response recommends the min_max integration helper with type: mean
Response does NOT recommend a template sensor (YAML or UI helper) as the primary solution

evals.prompt: create an automation that turns on the lights when I get home
evals.expected_output: Uses state trigger with to: home on a person entity. Does NOT use the removed entered_home device trigger type.

assertions.description: Response uses a state trigger (trigger: state) on a person or device_tracker entity, to: home
Response does NOT use the entered_home device trigger type (removed in 2026.5)

evals.prompt: make an automation that announces on my speaker when my kitchen timer finishes
evals.expected_output: Uses timer.finished event trigger. Does NOT use a polling template or state trigger workaround.

assertions.description: Response uses event trigger with event_type: timer.finished (or purpose-specific timer trigger)
Response does NOT suggest polling or a wait_template workaround for detecting timer completion

evals.prompt: add a card to my dashboard to quickly launch Assist
evals.expected_output: Recommends the shortcut card (new in 2026.5) for launching Assist from a dashboard.

assertions.description: Response recommends the shortcut card type for launching Assist
Response does not exclusively recommend a button card with tap_action when shortcut is more appropriate
